name: Easter Eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the powdered sugar and butter in a large bowl until combined.
2. Add the chopped nuts, maraschino cherries, pineapple, and coconut to the mixture. Stir until evenly distributed.
3. Sprinkle salt over the mixture and stir gently to combine.
4. Gradually add milk, stirring continuously until the mixture is thick enough to hold its shape but not overly stiff.
5. Shape the mixture into egg-shaped forms on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or wax paper.
6. Allow the shapes to dry completely at room temperature before proceeding.
7. Melt chocolate chips in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally until smooth and glossy.
8. If desired, add shaved paraffin to the melted chocolate for an extra shine.
9. Dip each dried shape into the melted chocolate, ensuring it is fully coated on all sides.
10. Place the dipped shapes back onto the baking sheet lined with parchment or wax paper.
11. Allow the chocolate to set completely at room temperature before personalizing and decorating with powdered sugar icing.
12. Once the chocolate has set, use your favorite Easter-themed decorations to customize each egg as desired.
